agents.autoRestart.fresh: Fresh — drop context (this speeds it up)
agents.autoRestart.note: Long sessions slow down; periodic restart keeps the session fresh (defaults to after the nightly consolidation). The main channels session always restarts fresh; it never interrupts running work, only restarts when idle.

agents.avatar.uploadDeferred: Image upload is configured on the host (the preset color applies for now).

agents.settings.hubReadonly: Main-agent config — read-only. Identity files can't be edited from the dashboard (token-leak risk); edit them on disk or via chat.
agents.settings.mcpHubReadonly: The main agent's .mcp.json may carry secrets, so it is read-only here (SPEC §8).

agents.team.autoDelegate: Auto-delegation: the leader splits and assigns the task itself
agents.team.autoDelegateNote: If unchecked, the leader only proposes a plan; you approve it via the channel (Telegram).
agents.team.hubNote: The orchestrator is the root of the graph — no editable team form.
agents.team.reportsNote: Left empty, it reports to the main agent automatically.
agents.team.trustNote: By default the message router decides whether an incoming message is from a trusted teammate based on the report-to and delegation relationships. Here you can manually mark additional agents as trusted — e.g. a cross-team collaborator with no hierarchical link, whose messages should still count as coming from a teammate.

connectors.builtin.browser.body: A native runtime browser-control capability, not a registered MCP server. The dashboard cannot auto-detect it; enable it per the upstream docs.
connectors.builtin.computer.body: This is a native runtime capability, not a manageable MCP server. The dashboard cannot auto-detect it. Enable it with a launch flag; use with care — it grants full screen access.
connectors.envPrompt.note: This MCP server requires the env vars below. The values are stored encrypted in the Vault.
connectors.envPrompt.title: Enter API keys
connectors.gallery.manageElsewhere: Manage it on the Installed tab (or upstream)
connectors.infoBanner: The listed MCP servers are the ones enabled in your Claude subscription (OAuth connectors, marketplace plugins, local config). Every sub-agent sees the global ones automatically because they share the same HOME. Do not re-add locally something already enabled upstream (it duplicates and the CLI warns "local wins"). The list loads at dashboard start and refreshes only on demand via Refresh.
connectors.test.invalid: Fill in the URL (HTTP/SSE) or command (stdio) first
connectors.test.refusedInternal: Refused — that address is internal (SSRF guard). Allowlist it in browse_ssrf_allow to probe it.
connectors.howto.title: How to wire your own MCP server
connectors.howto.intro: An MCP server gives agents extra tools. There are two standard shapes:
connectors.howto.stdio: stdio — a local command the agent spawns and talks to over stdin/stdout. Give the command (e.g. npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem) and any args. The command runs on THIS host, so it must be installed/on PATH (use Test to check).
connectors.howto.http: HTTP/SSE — a remote server reached over a URL. Give the https:// endpoint; add auth as an env var/header if needed.
connectors.howto.secrets: Secrets: add the env var NAME here; the VALUE goes to the Vault and is never stored or logged with the connector. Test never sends your secrets.
connectors.howto.assign: Assign: a project-scoped connector can be assigned to specific agents; the hub always has access. Use the per-connector Enable/Disable toggle to turn one off without deleting it.
connectors.howto.examples: Examples — stdio: command npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem, args /data. HTTP: url https://mcp.example.com/mcp with a bearer token in an env var.
connectors.install.title: Install {icon} {name}
connectors.stale: Refresh failed: {error} -- the claude.ai connectors may be stale.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.subtitle: Manage inbound triggers and outbound targets
webhooks.infoBanner: Inbound webhooks authenticate with a per-hook HMAC secret (not the dashboard bearer). Outbound targets are allowlisted, named URLs — the SSRF guard blocks localhost/private/metadata addresses.
webhooks.allowlistNote: Exact hostnames (one per line or comma-separated) outbound webhooks may reach despite the private-range block.
webhooks.hmacNote: The sender signs the raw body with this secret (x-webhook-signature, SHA-256 hex). Stored in the vault, never shown.
webhooks.action.label: Action
webhooks.fieldsNote: One per line: field=$.json.path (or =literal). e.g. title=$.issue.title. NO code from the payload is ever executed.

plugins.agentNote: Manage each agent's native Claude Code plugins: add a marketplace, then enable plugins per agent. Apply restarts the agent so it reloads them. A plugin never grants more than the agent's security profile allows.
plugins.applyHint: Restarts the agent (if running) so Claude Code reloads its plugins.
plugins.applyNoop: Agent is not running — nothing to restart.
plugins.noBrowsable: No browseable plugins. Add a local marketplace, or enable one by name below.
plugins.noneSelected: Select at least one plugin first.
plugins.marketUnbrowsable: This marketplace can't be browsed here ({name}) — add a plugin by name below.
plugins.marketEmpty: This marketplace has no plugins to enable here ({name}) — add one by name below.
plugins.extInfo: Extension plugins extend the orchestrator itself (views, routes, scheduled tasks, agent tools). They are disabled by default and run with a restricted host API — no billing change, no vault, no privilege escalation.

schedules.subtitle: Manage scheduled tasks
schedules.templatePrompt.calendar: Check today's calendar. If a meeting is within an hour, ping via Telegram and remind again 10 minutes before. If nothing is upcoming, stay silent.
schedules.templatePrompt.email: Check email from the last hour. If an urgent/important message arrives (client, boss, payment-related), ping via Telegram. Ignore promos and newsletters.
schedules.templatePrompt.full: Combined check: a calendar event within an hour + an urgent email in the last hour + a kanban card due today. Ping concisely via Telegram only if anything matters; otherwise stay quiet.
schedules.templatePrompt.kanban: Check the kanban board. If any card is due today, or is urgent and not done, ping via Telegram; otherwise stay silent.
schedules.title: Schedules
settings.appearanceDesc: Theme, density, glow, accent — per-browser, instant.
settings.billing.confirm: Switch to API billing? Agents will bill pay-as-you-go against your anthropic_api_key — this costs real money per token. The subscription default never bills you accidentally.
settings.billing.desc: Subscription (default) uses your shared Max OAuth — nobody pays accidentally. API is an explicit opt-in for buyers without a subscription; nothing ever switches to API automatically (not even on quota exhaustion).
settings.billing.keyHint: Add an anthropic_api_key in the Vault first — API mode needs it.

settings.sharedAuth.desc: Shared-subscription agents all use the host's Claude login. Re-authenticate ONCE on the host (run `claude` on the host shell), then push it to every agent here — never log in per-agent.
settings.sharedAuth.expired: The host login has EXPIRED. Run `claude` login on the host first, then refresh.
settings.sharedAuth.hostHint: Step 1: on the host shell, run `claude` and complete the login. Step 2: click Refresh below to re-link + restart every shared agent.
settings.sharedAuth.missing: No host login found. Run `claude` login on the host, then refresh.

panel.title: Judge Panels
panel.subtitle: Debate → adversarial judge → pick-best, behind a gated apply
panel.confirm.apply: Apply the winning solution? This merges its branch after the gate.
panel.confirm.reject: Reject this panel? Branches are archived and the winner is told to stop.
wizard.title: Setup wizard
wizard.subtitle: Get your assistant working, step by step. Only the first step is required.
wizard.tokenNote: Stored encrypted in the vault — never shown again.
wizard.s1.title: Subscription login
wizard.s1.what: This is how your agents think — your Claude subscription. Required.
wizard.s1.instr:
In a terminal on this machine, run:

    claude login

It opens a browser — approve, and you are done. One login covers all your agents. Then click Re-check below.
wizard.s1.ok: Subscription login detected — your agents can think.
wizard.s1.notfound: Still not logged in — run `claude login` on this machine, then re-check.
wizard.s2.title: Chat channel
wizard.s2.what: Talk to your assistant from your phone via Telegram or Discord. Optional — the dashboard always works.
wizard.tg.title: Telegram
wizard.tg.instr:
1) In Telegram, open a chat with @BotFather.
2) Send /newbot, follow the prompts — it gives you a TOKEN. Copy it.
3) Send any message to your new bot, then open in a browser:
   https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates
   Find your CHAT ID (the number in "chat":{"id":…}).
4) Paste the token + chat id below, Save, then Test.
wizard.dc.title: Discord
wizard.dc.instr:
1) Go to discord.com/developers/applications → New Application → Bot.
2) Copy the BOT TOKEN.
3) Bot → Privileged Gateway Intents → turn ON "Message Content Intent".
4) Invite the bot to your server (OAuth2 URL, bot scope).
5) Enable Developer Mode (Settings → Advanced), then right-click your channel → Copy Channel ID.
6) Paste the token + channel id below, Save, then Test.
wizard.dc.intentWarn: Important: turn ON the "Message Content Intent" — without it the bot cannot read your messages (the #1 cause of "Discord receives nothing").
wizard.dc.ok: Connected — your Discord bot is reachable.
wizard.s3.title: Local AI model (memory)
wizard.s3.what: Optional. Adds semantic memory search and lets the local-model agents run. Works fine without it — memory just uses keyword search.
wizard.s3.instr:
1) Install ollama from ollama.com/download (one click for your OS).
2) In a terminal: ollama pull nomic-embed-text (for memory). Optional: ollama pull muse-brain:latest for the local image/video agents.
3) ollama runs at http://localhost:11434 by default — paste that URL (or your own) below, Save, then Test.
wizard.s3.fail: Couldn’t reach ollama ({state}) — check the URL / that it is running.
wizard.s4.title: Image & video generation
wizard.s4.what: Optional, and needs a graphics card (GPU). Lets the creative agents generate images and video via ComfyUI. Skip unless you have a GPU box — everything else works without it.
wizard.s4.instr:
1) On a machine with a GPU, install ComfyUI (github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI) and start it:
   python main.py --listen 0.0.0.0
2) It serves at http://<that-machine-ip>:8188 — paste that URL below.
3) (Optional) if it is a different computer you can give SSH details to wake it.
Save, then Test.
wizard.s4.ok: ComfyUI reachable — image/video generation is ready.
wizard.s4.fail: Couldn’t reach ComfyUI ({state}) — check the URL / that it is running.
wizard.finish.title: All set
wizard.finish.restartNote: Note: enabling a channel takes effect after the next restart of the service.
wizard.s1.apiTitle: Advanced: use your own API key instead
wizard.s1.apiInstr: Subscription is the recommended default. If you prefer your own Anthropic API key (metered, pay-as-you-go), paste it below and switch to API mode. You can switch back anytime. Takes effect after a restart.
wizard.s1.apiOn: API mode is on — your agents think via your Anthropic API key.
wizard.s1.okApi: API mode active — your agents can think via your API key.

team.graphAria: Relationship graph (reportsTo hierarchy). The accessible equivalent is the Agents list viewpoint.
